title: Fix gate pass success reporting for failed automated gates
description:
Fix confusing `jit gate pass` behavior so a failed automated gate cannot look like a successful operation.

## Background

A critical CLI UX confusion was reported: `jit gate pass` can appear successful even when the underlying gate outcome, such as an AI code review gate, is failed. Users and agents need command success/failure, human output, and JSON output to distinguish “the command ran” from “the gate passed.”

## Success Criteria

- [ ] `jit gate pass` for an automated gate with a failing checker exits non-zero.
- [ ] Human-readable output clearly states that the gate failed and does not use success-shaped wording.
- [ ] JSON output includes the gate key, failed status, checker result details, and an actionable remediation hint.
- [ ] Manual gate pass behavior remains successful when the manual attestation is accepted.
- [ ] Tests cover a failed automated gate and confirm the command does not appear successful.
